selected_important_turn.text: I need 3 pack of fire wood and 2 pack of food and 2 pack of water
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first concrete, rigid demand from YOU and it shifts the conversation from the opponent's cooperative question into a self-focused, zero-sum frame. That early move anchors expectations and triggers defensive/protective responses from THEM.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: The demand is unilateral and absolute (specific high asks for all items) rather than opening tradeoffs or exploring what the opponent can spare. It misses an early opportunity to reveal preferences, propose integrative trade-offs, or build reciprocity; as a result it escalates competition and reduces joint-options.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Shift from an absolute claim to a collaborative request: explain priorities succinctly, ask what the other can spare, or propose explicit trades (e.g., I most need firewood — what can we swap so you keep what you need?). Use questions and options to open integrative possibilities instead of a fixed take-it-or-leave-it demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (opening tradeoffs creates more Pareto options)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (more transparent exchange could distribute items according to needs)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (less defensive responses from THEM, faster concessions through trade)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve (less zero-sum tone, more cooperative rapport)

value2reason.Medium: We are 4 in our family so I need extra food.
value2reason.High: I need firewood because it is used for cooking food and boiling water.

value2reason.Low: its not imporant then other 2 of the pack so its lowest
value2reason.Medium: its neccessary for all so its second priority
value2reason.High: its very imporant for the trip so its high priority

dialogue.text: hai friend, me and my team going to trip on some snow fall place i want some help from you
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Yeah sure. I do my level best .
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: okay friend. In my team old age sicked peoples and kids are there so i need some extra package.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay friend. Which item do you need the most?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 3 pack of fire wood and 2 pack of food and 2 pack of water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I value firewood the most for the trip. What can we swap so you keep what you need? I’m open to trading food or water for firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need firewood for cooking and boiling water, which are high priority for me. I’m willing to trade 1 food package for 1 firewood package. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages to keep the campsite warm. I’ll give you 1 food package for 1 firewood, or 2 water packages for 1 firewood. What works for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m willing to trade 1 food for 1 firewood, but I need at least 2 firewood packages. If you give me 2 firewood, I’ll give you 2 food. Let’s make it fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages to keep the campsite warm. If you give me 2 firewood, I’ll give you 2 food. Let’s make it fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 firewood packages. If you give me 2 firewood, I’ll give you 2 food. Let’s make it fair.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need 2 firewood packages to stay warm. If you give me 2 firewood, I’ll give you 2 food. Let’s finalize this deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
